visa_free.notes: Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau
Visa exemption only for holders of Machine-Readable Passport (MRP) in compliance with ICAO standards (since April 1, 2010). Those without an MRP are advised to obtain a visa in advance.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since September 30, 2023). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Short-term stay visa exemption; maximum stay is 30 days
Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau

Visa exemption only for holders of Special Administrative Region (SAR) passport issued by the Hong Kong SAR of the People's Republic of China, or British National Overseas (BNO) passports who have the right of residence in Hong Kong

Visa exemption only for holders of ICAO-compliant ePassport registered with a diplomatic mission of Japan in Indonesia (since December 1, 2014). Registration valid 3 years or until passport expires. Maximum stay is 15 days.
Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau
Visa exemption only for holders of Machine-Readable Passport (MRP) in compliance with ICAO standards (since April 1, 2010). Those without an MRP are advised to obtain a visa in advance.
Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau
Visa exemption only for holders of SAR passport issued by the Macao SAR of the People's Republic of China

Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since July 1, 2013). Those without an ePassport are advised to obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be strictly examined and may be refused entry.
Short-term stay visa exemption; may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since September 1, 2025). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since April 1, 2024). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since June 1, 2025). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since July 1, 2025). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.

Visa exemption only for holders of ICAO-compliant ePassport registered with a diplomatic mission of Japan (since April 2, 2023). Registration valid 3 years or until passport expires. Maximum stay is 30 days.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since May 1, 2011). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau
Visa exemption limited to passport holders with a personal ID number

Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since July 1, 2013); maximum stay is 15 days. Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.

Visa exemption only for holders of Machine-Readable Passport (MRP) in compliance with ICAO standards (since April 1, 2011). Those without an MRP are advised to obtain a visa in advance. Official MOFA name is 'Türkiye'.
Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since November 1, 2022). Those without an ePassport must obtain a visa in advance, otherwise will be refused entry.
Short-term stay visa exemption; nationals may apply for extension up to 6 months at Regional Immigration Bureau

Short-term stay visa exemption. IMPORTANT RESTRICTION (Note 11): New Uruguayan ordinary passports issued after April 16, 2025 that do not include a 'place of birth' on the personal data page are NOT recognized as valid by the Japanese government and are not eligible for visa-free travel. Uruguayan nationals holding old-version passports (issued before April 16, 2025, or new passports that still include 'place of birth') remain eligible for visa exemption.

e_visa.notes: Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA. Applies to foreign nationals residing in Australia who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA for those who do not qualify for visa exemption (no ICAO ePassport). Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA. Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA for those who do not qualify for visa exemption. Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA. Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA. Ordinary passport holders only.

Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA for those who do not qualify for visa exemption (no personal ID number in passport). Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA for those who do not qualify for visa exemption. Ordinary passport holders only.
Direct online application via JAPAN eVISA website. Single-entry short-term stay (tourism) eVISA for those who do not qualify for visa exemption. Ordinary passport holders only.

eVISA via accredited agency only. Nationals of China residing in China. Period of stay is '15 days' or '30 days'. Must apply through a travel agency accredited by the Japanese overseas establishment. Ordinary passport holders only.

eVISA via accredited agency only. Nationals of the Philippines residing in the Philippines who participate in a packaged tour organized by designated travel agencies. Period of stay is '15 days'. Ordinary passport holders only.

eVISA via accredited agency only. Nationals of Vietnam residing in Vietnam who participate in a packaged tour organized by designated travel agencies. Period of stay is '15 days'. Ordinary passport holders only.

eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in Hong Kong who are not exempt from short-term visa. Applies to SAR passport holders who do not qualify for the ordinary visa exemption. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in India who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in Indonesia who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in Macao who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in Mongolia who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.

eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in the Republic of Korea (except jurisdiction over the Consulate General of Japan in Jeju) who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in Singapore who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.
eVISA via accredited agency. All foreign nationals/people residing in UAE who are not exempt from short-term visa. Ordinary passport holders only.

visa_policy.notes: Japan has 74 visa-exempt countries/regions as of September 1, 2025 per MOFA. Stay limits: 15 days (Indonesia, Thailand), 30 days (Brunei, Qatar), 90 days for all others. Several countries require ICAO-compliant ePassport (Note 2: Montenegro, Peru, Paraguay, Panama, Brazil, UAE, Thailand, Serbia) or registered ePassport (Indonesia, Qatar) or MRP (Barbados, Turkey, Lesotho). Malaysia requires ePassport (advised). Uruguay ordinary visa exemption does NOT apply to new passports issued after April 16, 2025 that omit 'place of birth'. JAPAN eVISA system (as of May 15, 2026): expanded to 19 eligible countries/regions. Group 1 (direct online apply): Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, UK, USA. Group 2 (via accredited agency only): China (15/30-day stay), Philippines (15 days, packaged tour), Vietnam (15 days, packaged tour), Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Macao, Mongolia, Republic of Korea (excl. Jeju jurisdiction), Singapore, UAE. Japan does not offer visa-on-arrival or ETA programs.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Bangladesh must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only, maximum 30 days. Reciprocal diplomatic visa exemption arrangement came into effect November 15, 2016. Ordinary passport holders of Mongolia must obtain a visa.

Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of India must obtain a visa (India not on the ordinary visa exemption list). Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of the Philippines must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Myanmar must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Albania must obtain a visa (not on the ordinary visa exemption list). Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Azerbaijan must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only, maximum 90 days. Reciprocal diplomatic visa exemption arrangement came into effect June 1, 2015. Ordinary passport holders of Georgia must obtain a visa.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Moldova must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Turkmenistan must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Ukraine must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Uzbekistan must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Kosovo must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of the Kyrgyz Republic must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Kazakhstan must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only under the dedicated diplomatic/official arrangement. Note: Vatican City ordinary passport holders are also visa-exempt separately.
Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Ecuador must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Bolivia must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Colombia are advised to obtain a visa in advance (since February 1, 2004), otherwise subject to strict examination. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.
Diplomatic passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders of Angola must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Bahrain must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Kuwait must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Morocco must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Oman must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official/special passport holders (Saudi Arabia's special passports are treated as official). Reciprocal arrangement came into effect January 2, 2026. Ordinary passport holders of Saudi Arabia may use eVISA.

Diplomatic and official passport holders only. The ordinary passport visa exemption for Iran has been suspended since April 15, 1992. Only diplomatic and official passport holders may enter Japan as Temporary Visitor for up to 90 days without a visa.

Diplomatic and official passport holders, maximum 30 days. Reciprocal arrangement came into effect July 20, 2016. Ordinary Indonesian passport holders have separate visa exemption arrangement (15-day ePassport registration system).

Diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport holders of Bosnia and Herzegovina must obtain a visa (not on ordinary visa exemption list). Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Diplomatic and official passport holders of these Oceanian states. Ordinary passport holders must obtain a visa. Covered under Japan's reciprocal Visa Exemption Arrangements for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders.

Holders of a valid United Nations laissez-passer AND a passport from a country listed in the MOFA UNLP exemption table, visiting Japan for official purpose of the United Nations. Scope mirrors the diplomatic/official passport exemption arrangements. Must hold both a UNLP and a home-country passport. Some countries require UNLP with red cover only (e.g. Bangladesh, India, Mongolia, Philippines, Myanmar).

cbi.notes: Japan does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. Japanese citizenship is acquired by birth, notification, or naturalization only (Japanese Nationality Act, Law No. 147 of 1950). Naturalization generally requires 5 or more consecutive years of legal residence, age 18+, good conduct, and ability to support oneself. Japan does not allow permanent dual citizenship; those acquiring Japanese citizenship must renounce other nationalities. Source: Ministry of Justice, https://www.moj.go.jp/EN/MINJI/minji78.html

programs.name: Business Manager Residence Status
programs.notes: Allows foreign nationals to manage or operate businesses in Japan. As of October 2025, minimum capital or total investment raised from JPY 5 million to JPY 30 million. Also requires: at least 1 full-time employee resident in Japan with Japanese language ability, and applicant must have either 3+ years business management experience or a master's/doctoral degree in a related field, plus a business plan reviewed by a specialist. Path to permanent residence depends on points and conduct. Path to citizenship is standard naturalization (5 years residence). Source: Immigration Services Agency / Baker McKenzie analysis of ISA announcement August 2025.

programs.name: Highly Skilled Foreign Professional (HSP) - Points-Based
programs.notes: Points-based system. Foreign nationals scoring 70+ points (academic background, professional career, annual income) are admitted as Highly Skilled Foreign Professionals. Those scoring 80+ points are eligible for permanent residence after just 1 year of residence. Three activity categories: (i) Advanced Academic Research, (ii) Advanced Specialized/Technical, (iii) Advanced Business Management. Source: MOFA/ISA.

programs.name: Special Highly-Skilled Professional (J-Skip)
programs.notes: Introduced April 2023. Granted to foreign nationals meeting high academic/income standards without needing to calculate HSP points. For professionals/researchers: master's degree or higher (or 10+ years experience) AND annual income of at least JPY 20 million. For business managers: 5+ years management experience AND annual income of at least JPY 40 million. Eligible for permanent residence after 1 year. Source: Immigration Services Agency of Japan (J-Skip PDF).

programs.name: J-Find (Future Creation Individual Visa)
programs.eligibility: Graduates (within 5 years) of universities ranked in the top 100 by at least 2 of 3 major rankings (QS, THE, ARWU). As of January 2025, 94 universities qualify. Minimum savings of JPY 200,000 at time of application. Age 18+.
programs.notes: Designated Activities No. 51 (worker), No. 52 (dependent). Allows stay of up to 2 years for job hunting or entrepreneurship preparation in Japan without a pre-arranged job offer. Introduced April 2023 alongside J-Skip. Employment in adult entertainment establishments not permitted. Source: MOFA and Consulate-General of Japan in Denver.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa (Designated Activities No. 53/54)
programs.eligibility: Nationals of visa-exempt countries/regions (approximately 70 countries). Must be employed by or provide services to entities/organizations established outside Japan. Annual income of at least JPY 10 million. Must hold sufficient private medical insurance (minimum JPY 10 million per person). Cannot work for Japanese entities.
programs.notes: Launched March 31, 2024. Designated Activities No. 53 for worker, No. 54 for accompanying spouse/child. Maximum stay of 6 months; must wait 6 months before reapplying for the same status. Entry by air or sea (including scheduled ferries Japan–Busan or Japan–Shanghai). Does not permit engagement with Japanese employers or clients. Source: MOFA Japan.

programs.name: Start-up Visa
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals supported by designated Japanese municipalities for starting a business in Japan. Spouse and children of the entrepreneur are also eligible.
programs.notes: Visa applicant must conform to the conditions provided by Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry Order. Application requires Certificate of Eligibility (COE). Allows foreign entrepreneurs to reside in Japan to prepare for business start-up with support from a designated municipality. Source: MOFA Japan.

programs.name: Specified Skilled Worker (SSW)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with specific industry skills and knowledge. Covers 14 specified industry sectors. Must pass skills and Japanese language tests (SSW1) or have Technical Intern Training experience. SSW2 available in select sectors.
programs.notes: Specified Skilled Worker No. 1 (SSW1): up to 5 years, no family accompaniment in most sectors. Specified Skilled Worker No. 2 (SSW2): indefinitely renewable, family accompaniment permitted, path to permanent residence. Source: MOFA Japan.

outbound_official.notes: Official MOFA outbound visa list for Japanese ordinary passport holders (普通旅券). Primary source is the PDF at https://www.mofa.go.jp/mofaj/files/100804073.pdf (current as of March 12, 2026; 令和８年３月１２日現在). The HTML page is https://www.mofa.go.jp/mofaj/toko/visa/tanki/novisa.html. Note: Emergency passports (緊急旅券) do not include IC chips and may face additional travel restrictions. The visa-free list represents countries/territories where Japanese ordinary passport holders can enter without obtaining a prior consular visa (may still require eTA/ESTA pre-travel authorization which is obtained online without visiting a consulate). Some destinations listed under both visa_free and e_visa/eta indicate that the entry mode is eTA/e-visa (electronic, no consulate visit required). Countries with VOA also allow entry without prior consular visa. This list is approximate; the definitive authoritative list is in the MOFA PDF. Japan passport holders have access to 190+ destinations without a prior consular visa appointment.

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1 - Malaysia ePassport requirement added: Previous record listed Malaysia as a plain 'Short-term stay visa exemption' with no condition. Official MOFA novisa.html (Note 3) clearly states the exemption is limited to ePassport holders since July 1, 2013; non-ePassport holders are advised to obtain a visa in advance. Added ePassport condition to Malaysia's visa_free entry.

CORRECTION 2 - Uruguay passport restriction added: Previous record listed Uruguay with no caveats. Official MOFA novisa.html (Note 11, new) states that new Uruguayan ordinary passports issued after April 16, 2025 that omit 'place of birth' from the personal data page are NOT recognized by Japan and are not eligible for visa-free travel. Added critical restriction to Uruguay's notes.

CORRECTION 3 - Qatar ePassport registration requirement added: Previous record noted Qatar as '30 days' with only a stay-limit note. Official MOFA novisa.html (Note 9) states Qatar nationals must register their ICAO-compliant ePassport with a Japanese diplomatic mission (valid 3 years). Updated Qatar notes to reflect registration requirement (same system as Indonesia).

CORRECTION 4 - Indonesia notes updated to reflect registration system: The previous record said 'Visa exemption only for holders of ePassport in compliance with ICAO standards (since December 1, 2014)' but omitted that Indonesia operates a pre-registration system at Japanese diplomatic missions in Indonesia. Updated to match the official Note 1 language.

CORRECTION 5 - Barbados and Lesotho notes corrected: Previous record said 'Visa exemption only for holders of Machine-Readable Passport (MRP)' without specifying the advisory-not-mandatory nature for non-MRP holders. Updated to match Note 7 wording: those without MRP are 'advised to obtain a visa in advance'.
CORRECTION 6 - Turkey name: Official MOFA page uses 'Türkiye'. Added note in the record. iso3 TUR unchanged.

CORRECTION 7 - Bosnia and Herzegovina added to conditional_access: The diplomatic/official passport exemption table at mofa.go.jp/ca/fna/page22e_000692.html lists 'Bosnia and Herzegovina' as a full diplomatic and official passport exempt country. This entry was entirely missing from the previous record's conditional_access section.

CORRECTION 8 - eVISA section massively expanded: Previous record listed 9 countries in e_visa (direct-apply only: Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, UK, USA). The official JAPAN eVISA page (dated May 15, 2026) confirms the system has expanded to 19 countries/regions total. Added 10 new agency-mediated eVISA entries: China (15/30-day stays), Philippines (15-day packaged tour), Vietnam (15-day packaged tour), Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Macao, Mongolia, Republic of Korea (excl. Jeju), Singapore, UAE.

CORRECTION 9 - eVISA notes updated for China/Philippines/Vietnam stay limits: The prior record showed all eVISA as '90 days'. For China, the period is '15 days' or '30 days'; for Philippines and Vietnam (packaged tour), the period is '15 days'. Updated max_stay_days accordingly.
VERIFIED - 74 visa-free countries/regions confirmed against the live MOFA novisa.html (September 1, 2025 version).
VERIFIED - Diplomatic/official passport exemptions: 65 countries confirmed against live page (January 2, 2026 version). Saudi Arabia arrangement (effective January 2, 2026) confirmed present.
VERIFIED - No credential-based (US/Schengen visa holder) conditional entry rule exists for Japan per official sources.
VERIFIED - CBI: no program confirmed. RBI programs and fast_track programs not updated as no contradicting official sources found in this session.

RE-VERIFICATION 2026-06-27 - Visa-free list: MOFA novisa.html confirmed still at 74 countries/regions as of September 1, 2025 effective date. No new visa-free additions detected between October 2025 and June 2026. All search results consistently cite the same 74-country list with Montenegro as the most recent addition (September 1, 2025). Direct WebFetch of mofa.go.jp returned HTTP 403; confirmed via WebSearch snippets citing MOFA directly and migaku.com cross-reference. No changes made to visa_free list.

RE-VERIFICATION 2026-06-27 - eVISA: No new eVISA-eligible countries identified since the May 15, 2026 expansion. The 19 countries/regions listed remain current. Direct WebFetch of mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/visaonline.html returned HTTP 403; confirmed via search snippets.

CORRECTION 2026-06-27 - Visa issuance fees updated: Japan increased visa fees for the first time in 48 years, effective July 1, 2026. Single-entry visa: JPY 3,000 → JPY 15,000 (~USD 100). Multiple-entry visa: JPY 6,000 → JPY 30,000 (~USD 200). Updated fee_usd in Temporary Visitor Visa (Tourist) from 25 to 100, and Short-Term Business Visa from 50 to 200. Source: deeparrival.com/news/japan-visa-fee-increase-2026/ (citing official government announcement).

NOTED 2026-06-27 - JESTA (Japan Electronic System for Travel Authorization): Japan's parliament passed the JESTA enabling law on May 29, 2026 (House of Councillors). The system will require visa-exempt travelers to obtain pre-travel electronic authorization, modeled after the US ESTA. Implementation is targeted for fiscal year 2028 (no earlier than April 2028, no later than March 2029). JESTA is NOT in effect as of June 2026 and does NOT change current entry rules. Approximately 31 million annual travelers will eventually fall within scope. The fee has not been finalized (officials referenced JPY 2,000–3,000 range). No changes to visa_policy made as a result. Source: visasnews.com reporting on parliamentary proceedings April–May 2026.

RE-VERIFICATION 2026-06-27 - Diplomatic/official passport exemptions: Not re-fetched (HTTP 403 on mofa.go.jp/ca/fna/page22e_000692.html). Carried forward from prior session. Search snippets confirm Saudi Arabia arrangement (effective January 2, 2026) remains the most recent diplomatic exemption addition. No new diplomatic arrangements detected.

RE-VERIFICATION 2026-06-27 - MOFA confirmed via WebSearch that no visa-free countries were added after September 1, 2025. No credential-based conditional entry found. eVISA list confirmed unchanged. visa_required field remains absent (correct: Japan does not operate a 'visa-free for all EXCEPT list' policy).

RE-VERIFICATION 2026-06-29 - Full re-check against official sources. MOFA novisa.html and mofa.go.jp still return HTTP 403 on direct WebFetch; confirmed via WebSearch snippets citing MOFA directly (migaku.com May 12 2026, Wikipedia, japan-visa.com). Visa-free list remains at 74 countries/regions as of September 1, 2025. No new additions since Montenegro (September 1, 2025) detected. Colombia confirmed NOT on ordinary visa-free list (exemption cancelled February 1, 2004) — current record is correct. Thailand ePassport requirement confirmed. eVISA system confirmed at 19 countries/regions (9 direct-apply + 10 agency-only) as of June 25, 2026 per mailmate.jp cross-reference. Visa fee increase (effective July 1, 2026): ¥3,000 → ¥15,000 single-entry, ¥6,000 → ¥30,000 multiple-entry now confirmed by Cabinet decision dated June 19, 2026 per Fragomen immigration alert (June 23, 2026), Euronews, Japan Times — no longer flagged as gap. JESTA: law passed House of Councillors May 29, 2026; target implementation FY2028 (April 2028 – March 2029); fee range ¥2,000–¥3,000 referenced but not finalized; NOT in effect for 2026 travel. No changes to visa_policy, conditional_access, or cbi/rbi/fast_track sections — all confirmed current.
gaps: Type A (credential-based conditional entry): No official MOFA source confirms that Japan grants visa-free/VoA/eVisa entry to any traveller solely because they hold a valid US, Schengen, EU, UK, Canada, Australia, or other third-country visa or residence permit. The Embassy of Japan in the US explicitly states 'The Visa Waiver Program applies based on your nationality, NOT on your U.S. residence status.'

Type B (OCI / special status): No official MOFA, MOJ, or ISA source references OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card or any analogous diaspora/heritage status as a basis for conditional entry into Japan.
Business Manager visa capital requirement of JPY 30 million sourced from Baker McKenzie analysis of ISA August 2025 announcement; the ISA official URL redirected to a generic page.
J-Skip and J-Find details sourced from Consulate-General of Japan in Denver (official embassy domain) and MOFA search snippets; ISA PDF was unreadable binary in previous session.
Japan has no freedom-of-movement agreements comparable to Schengen or CARICOM; ASEAN/RCEP/APEC memberships do not confer free movement of persons.
Processing times for most long-stay visas not found verbatim on official pages.
The exact list of countries eligible for the Digital Nomad Visa (approximately 70 visa-exempt countries) was not enumerated individually on the official MOFA page.

The exact list of countries covered under the UNLP exemption arrangements (mofa.go.jp/ca/fna/page22e_000930.html) was not re-fetched in this session; the existing entry is carried over from prior session which confirmed it mirrors the diplomatic/official passport arrangement.

Uruguay restriction (Note 11): It is unclear whether new Uruguayan passports issued after April 16, 2025 that DO include 'place of birth' remain fully eligible. The MOFA note states 'old version of Uruguayan passports' are eligible even if issued after April 16, 2025, implying the distinction is the presence of 'place of birth', not solely the date of issue.

Japan visa fee increase (July 1, 2026): RESOLVED 2026-06-29. Cabinet decision June 19, 2026 confirmed by Fragomen immigration alert (June 23, 2026), Euronews (June 22, 2026), Japan Times (June 20, 2026), and Newland Chase. Single-entry: ¥15,000; multiple-entry: ¥30,000. Effective July 1, 2026 for applications submitted on or after that date. Original gap (non-official deeparrival.com source) is now superseded by professional immigration firm citations and major media citing the cabinet meeting. No direct MOFA/MOJ URL confirmed as mofa.go.jp returns HTTP 403.

JESTA fee: The final authorization fee for JESTA has not been confirmed by any official government announcement as of June 2026. Parliamentary discussions referenced approximately JPY 2,000–6,000 but this is not official.

The exact list of countries eligible for Japan's Digital Nomad Visa (Designated Activities No. 53/54) remains not individually enumerated on the official MOFA page - only described as 'approximately 70 visa-exempt countries.' The visa_types notes says '49 countries with tax treaties' which may be outdated or incorrect; tax-treaty requirement is not mentioned on MOFA official pages. This discrepancy should be resolved in a future session.

COUNT DISCREPANCY 2026-06-29: The visa_free array contains 75 unique entries, but MOFA officially states 74 countries/regions (as of September 1, 2025). No duplicates exist in the array. The discrepancy could be: (a) MOFA counts Hong Kong and Macao as a combined SARs entry in their display count, (b) one entry in the file was added that is not on MOFA's official list, or (c) MOFA's published count differs from their actual list size. Direct WebFetch to MOFA novisa.html returns HTTP 403, preventing definitive resolution. Under the safety rule (preservation beats guessing), no entry has been removed. The previous session's validation note claims '74 confirmed against live MOFA novisa.html' but the file has 75 entries — this inconsistency should be resolved when MOFA direct access is restored.

EVISA COUNT 2026-06-29: The e_visa array contains 20 entries but the official MOFA eVISA system lists 19 eligible countries/regions (9 direct-apply + 10 agency-mediated as of May 15, 2026). The file has 11 agency-mediated entries (China, Philippines, Vietnam, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Macao, Mongolia, Republic of Korea, Singapore, UAE) while mailmate.jp (June 25, 2026) lists 10 without Singapore. The Singapore e_visa entry covers non-Singapore-national residents of Singapore applying via accredited agency. This is plausible as the eVISA accepts applications from RESIDENTS of eligible jurisdictions (not just nationals). However, Singapore is not listed as a separate eVISA jurisdiction in non-official cross-references. Under preservation rule, the Singapore entry is retained but flagged for verification when MOFA direct access is restored.

sources_checked.description: MOFA Japan - Exemption of Visa (Short-Term Stay) - official list of 74 visa-exempt countries (dated September 1, 2025)
sources_checked.notes: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy (June 2026). Full list confirmed including all notes on ePassport requirements, stay limits, and new Note 11 on Uruguay.

sources_checked.notes: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy. Confirmed expanded eVISA system to 19 countries/regions in two groups: direct online apply (9) and via accredited agency (10+ additional).

sources_checked.notes: Referenced but not re-fetched in this session; entry carried over from prior session.

sources_checked.notes: Confirmed no CBI program, dual citizenship not permitted (carried from prior session).

sources_checked.notes: HTTP 403 Forbidden on direct WebFetch 2026-06-27. Confirmed via WebSearch snippets from MOFA search results that list remains at 74 countries as of September 1, 2025. No changes to the list detected.

sources_checked.description: MOFA Japan - JAPAN eVISA system - re-check 2026-06-27
sources_checked.notes: HTTP 403 Forbidden on direct WebFetch 2026-06-27. WebSearch confirmed 19 countries/regions remain eligible as of May 15, 2026 update.

sources_checked.description: Deep Arrival - Japan Visa Fee Increase 2026 (non-official secondary source)
sources_checked.notes: Confirmed single-entry fee increase JPY 3,000 → JPY 15,000 and multiple-entry JPY 6,000 → JPY 30,000, effective July 1, 2026. NOT an official MOFA/MOJ domain - used because official MOFA page returned 403. Flagged as gap pending official confirmation.

sources_checked.description: VisasNews - JESTA parliamentary details (non-official secondary source)
sources_checked.notes: Confirmed JESTA law passed House of Councillors May 29, 2026. Target implementation FY2028. Not in effect 2026 or 2027. Current entry rules unchanged.

sources_checked.description: Migaku - Japan visa-free countries 2026 (non-official, cross-reference source, dated May 12, 2026)
sources_checked.notes: Re-verification 2026-06-29. Confirmed 74 visa-free countries as of September 1, 2025. No new additions detected. Colombia confirmed NOT on ordinary visa-free list (exemption cancelled February 1, 2004). Thailand ePassport requirement confirmed. All 90-day, 30-day, and 15-day country entries match current record.

sources_checked.description: MailMate - Japan eVISA guide (non-official, cross-reference source, last updated June 25, 2026)
sources_checked.notes: Re-verification 2026-06-29. Confirmed eVISA system remains at 19 countries/regions: 9 direct-apply (Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, UK, USA) + 10 agency-only (China, Vietnam, Philippines, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Macao, Mongolia, South Korea, UAE). No new countries added. Current record is accurate.

sources_checked.description: Fragomen immigration alert - Japan visa fee increase July 2026 (professional immigration firm, non-official)
sources_checked.notes: Re-verification 2026-06-29. Confirmed single-entry fee: ¥3,000 → ¥15,000; multiple-entry fee: ¥6,000 → ¥30,000, effective July 1, 2026. Cabinet decision dated June 19, 2026. Also notes proposed further increases for other immigration fees (e.g. permanent residence from ¥10,000 to ¥200,000) anticipated October 2026.

sources_checked.description: Wikipedia - Visa policy of Japan (non-official secondary source)
sources_checked.notes: Re-verification 2026-06-29. Cross-reference. Colombia confirmed not on visa-free list (exemption cancelled February 1, 2004). Most recent addition Montenegro September 1, 2025. 74 countries/regions total confirmed.

visa_types.name: Temporary Visitor Visa (Tourist)
visa_types.purpose: Sightseeing, visiting family, short-term business meetings, medical care
visa_types.notes: Apply at a Japanese embassy or consulate. Many nationalities are exempt. Single or double entry available. Fee JPY 15,000 (single entry) as of July 1, 2026 - increased from JPY 3,000. First fee increase in 48 years, effective for applications submitted on or after July 1, 2026. Source: deeparrival.com citing official announcement.

visa_types.name: Short-Term Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Business meetings, contract signing, market research, attending conferences
visa_types.notes: Multiple entry versions available for frequent business travellers. Apply at Japanese embassy/consulate. Fee JPY 30,000 (multiple entry) as of July 1, 2026 - increased from JPY 6,000.

visa_types.name: Engineer / Specialist in Humanities / International Services Visa
visa_types.purpose: Working in engineering, IT, finance, law, language education, or international trade
visa_types.notes: Requires a Certificate of Eligibility (CoE) obtained by your Japanese employer from immigration authorities. Most common work visa for office-based roles.

visa_types.name: Highly Skilled Professional Visa (HSP)
visa_types.purpose: Advanced academic research, specialised/technical activities, or business management
visa_types.notes: Points-based system. 70+ points qualifies for HSP. Accelerated path to permanent residence (3 years or 1 year at 80+ points).

visa_types.name: Student Visa (College Student / Pre-College Student)
visa_types.purpose: Studying at a Japanese school, university, language school, or vocational school
visa_types.notes: Requires a CoE from the school. Can work up to 28 hours/week part-time with a work permit. Apply at Japanese embassy/consulate.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa
visa_types.purpose: Holidaying and working in Japan (age 18–30, select nationalities)
visa_types.notes: Available for Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Ireland, New Zealand, South Korea, UK, and several others. Generally free of charge.

visa_types.name: Specified Skilled Worker Visa / Digital Nomad Visa
visa_types.purpose: Remote working while staying in Japan (launched March 2024)
visa_types.notes: For remote workers earning over JPY 10M/year (approx USD 70K). Available to nationals of 49 countries with tax treaties with Japan. Cannot work for Japanese companies.
